steps.title: Convert '129% greater' to a growth multiplier
steps.content:
If the mass is 129% greater than the previous period, the new mass equals the old mass plus 129% of the old mass:
$$\text{New} = \text{Old} + 1.29 \times \text{Old} = \text{Old} \times (1 + 1.29) = \text{Old} \times 2.29$$
The multiplier per 6-hour period is 2.29, not 1.29. This immediately eliminates choices A and D, which use base 1.29.

steps.title: Build the exponential model
steps.content:
With multiplier 2.29 every 6 hours and initial mass $A_0$:
$$A = A_0 \cdot (2.29)^{x/6}$$
where $x$ is time in hours. Choices B and C both use this base.

steps.title: Solve for the initial mass using the 18-hour condition
steps.content:
At $x = 18$: $A = 613.90$ grams.
$$613.90 = A_0 \cdot (2.29)^{18/6} = A_0 \cdot (2.29)^3$$
Compute $(2.29)^3$: $2.29^2 = 5.2441$; $5.2441 \times 2.29 \approx 12.009$.
$$A_0 = \frac{613.90}{12.009} \approx 51.12$$
Now check each remaining choice:
  • Choice A — 'A = 34.11(1.29)^{x/6}': Wrong base — 1.29 means 129% of the old mass, not 129% greater. At x = 18: $34.11 \times 1.29^3 = 34.11 \times 2.147 \approx 73.2$ grams, far below 613.90. ✗
  • Choice B — 'A = 34.11(2.29)^{x/6}': Correct base but wrong initial value. At x = 18: $34.11 \times 12.009 \approx 409.6$ grams, not 613.90. ✗
  • Choice C — 'A = 51.12(2.29)^{x/6}': Correct base and correct initial value. At x = 18: $51.12 \times 12.009 \approx 614.0 \approx 613.90$. ✓
  • Choice D — 'A = 285.98(1.29)^{x/6}': Wrong base (1.29 instead of 2.29). At x = 18: $285.98 \times 2.147 \approx 614.0$, but the growth factor is wrong — 129% greater means multiply by 2.29, not 1.29. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
